obsolescence_lever: Household incandescent bulbs that had often lasted 1500-2500 hours were standardized by cartel members toward 1000 hours.
labour_industry_channel: Stabilized replacement demand for manufacturers and upstream production while suppressing the long-life bulb path.
source_note: IEEE Spectrum reports cartel formation in 1924 and a 1000-hour lifetime standard; engineering trade-offs remain a caveat.

obsolescence_lever: Annual model changes and styling cycles made mechanically usable cars look out of date.
labour_industry_channel: Supported repeat showroom traffic, dealer networks and production smoothing; evidence is stronger for demand management than for deliberate physical failure.
source_note: Used as dynamic obsolescence rather than a proven reliability cap.

obsolescence_lever: Software updates reduced performance in some older iPhones with battery issues without adequate disclosure.
labour_industry_channel: Shows that capability can be throttled through software after sale; settlements addressed disclosure and consumer protection rather than proving a full planned-obsolescence model.
source_note: California DOJ announced a USD 113m multistate settlement over battery/performance throttling allegations.

obsolescence_lever: Parts, diagnostic software, firmware locks, adhesives and warranty practices can limit independent repair and reuse.
labour_industry_channel: Preserves authorized service and replacement demand; can also slow second-hand and refurbishment channels.
source_note: FTC's 2021 Nixing the Fix report examined repair restrictions and found scant evidence for many manufacturer justifications.

obsolescence_lever: Repair obligations and consumer repair rights directly counter premature disposal and repair deterrence.
labour_industry_channel: Shifts policy toward durability, repair markets and longer service lives rather than replacement-first cycles.
source_note: Directive adopted 13 June 2024 and entered into force 30 July 2024.
